LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JOHNA PECOT, THOMAS ARATA, RICH OWYANG, STEPHEN TILTON, JOSEPH LEAKE, OSCAR TAYLOR'S JOINDER TO DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY COUNSEL IS DENIED. SFDSA HAS NOT MET IS BURDEN TO DISQUALIFY MR. MURRAY AS COUNSEL. SEE STATE FARM MUT. AUTO. INS. CO. V. FEDERAL INS. CO. (1999) 72 CAL.APP.4TH 1422, 1431. IN ALL FOUR ACTIONS ("PECOT I," "PECOT II," THE FEDERAL LANHAM ACT ACTION, AND THE "CONVERSION" ACTION), SFDSA WAS REPRESENTED BY SEPARATE COUNSEL OTHER THAN MR. MURRAY. MR. MURRAY'S COMMUNICATIONS AND INVOICES ARE CONSISTENT WITH HIS REPRESENTATION OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS AND HIS BELIEF UNDER THE DISPUTED FEE AGREEMENT THAT SFDSA PAID FOR HIS SERVICES. FURTHER, SFDSA HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED A SUBSTANTIAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE SUBJECTS OF THE CURRENT REPRESENTATION OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS AND MR. MURRAY'S ALLEGED FORMER REPRESENTATION OF A SHERIFF IN AN ADMINISTRATIVE APPEAL AND IN A CORPORATE MATTER. FURTHER, SFDSA HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT MR. MURRAY HAD ACCESS TO CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION SUCH THAT IT IS A BASIS TO DISQUALIFY HIM AS COUNSEL IN THIS CASE. SFDSA HAS NOT SHOWN THAT MR. BOSWORTH'S REPRESENTATION OF THE FOUNDATION IS A BASIS TO DISQUALIFY MR. MURRAY IN THIS ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVID WONG'S MOTION PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473 (D) TO CORRECT CLERICAL ERROR IN ORDER AFTER DEMURRER AND SLAPP HEARING IS DENIED. THE JANUARY 12, 2015 ORDER ACCURATELY REFLECTS THE COURT'S RULING ON THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF STIPULATED TO REMOVING ALLEGATIONS PERTAINING TO THE DISMISSED DEFENDANTS ON THE RECORD AT THE HEARING, DEFENDANTS DID NOT PREVAIL ON THEIR ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. AS STATED IN THE ORDER, AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION CAN ONLY BE USED TO CHALLENGE CAUSES OF ACTION AND NOT SPECIFIC FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS. A REGULAR MOTION TO STRIKE WOULD HAVE BEEN THE PROPER PROCEDURAL MECHANISM FOR ACHIEVING THE RESULTS SOUGHT FROM THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. FURTHER, DEFENDANTS ENGAGED IN NO MEANINGFUL ANALYSIS AS TO WHETHER THE FIRST PRONG OF THE STATUTE WAS MET. THUS, THE ORDER IS ACCURATE, INCLUDING THE STATEMENT THAT THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION WAS FRIVOLOUS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473 (D) To Correct Clerical Error In Order After Demurrer And Slapp Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Paul Kranz, of Law Office of Paul L. Kranz, Tel. 510.839.1200, for plaintiffs;
Lawrence Murray, of Murray & Associates, Tel. 415.673.0555, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DAVID WONG'S Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473 (D) To Correct Clerical Error In Order After Demurrer And Slapp Hearing is denied. The January 12, 2015 Order accurately reflects the Court's ruling on the Anti-SLAPP motion. Although Plaintiff stipulated to removing allegations pertaining to the dismissed Defendants on the record at the hearing, Defendants did not prevail on their Anti-SLAPP motion. As stated in the Order, an Anti-SLAPP motion can only be used to challenge causes of action and not specific factual allegations. A regular motion to strike would have been the proper procedural mechanism for achieving the results sought from the Anti-SLAPP motion. Further, Defendants engaged in no meaningful analysis as to whether the first prong of the statute was met. Thus, the Order is accurate, including the statement that the Anti-SLAPP motion was frivolous. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Ntc Of Joinder And Joinder To Def San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs' Association'S Mtn To Disqualify Counsel
Off Calendar Jun-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: PAUL KRANZ, ESQ., 510.549.5900, FOR PLAINTIFF JOHNA PECOT, THOMAS ARATA, RICH OWYANG, STEVEN TILTON, JOSEPH LEAKE AND OSCAR TAYLOR.
LAWRENCE MURRAY, ESQ., 415.673.0555, FOR INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS.
LARA SMITH, ESQ., 510.919.3214, FOR DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS JOHNA PECOT, THOMAS ARATA, RICH OWYANG, STEPHEN TILTON, JOSEPH LEAKE, OSCAR TAYLOR'S Joinder To Defendant San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs' Association's Motion To Disqualify Counsel IS Denied. SFDSA has not met is burden to disqualify Mr. Murray as counsel. See State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. v. Federal Ins. Co. (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1431. In all four actions ("Pecot I," "Pecot II," the Federal Lanham Act action, and the "conversion" action), SFDSA was represented by separate counsel other than Mr. Murray. Mr. Murray's communications and invoices are consistent with his representation of the individual defendants and his belief under the disputed fee agreement that SFDSA paid for his services. Further, SFDSA has not demonstrated a substantial relationship between the subjects of the current representation of the individual defendants and Mr. Murray's alleged former representation of a sheriff in an administrative appeal and in a corporate matter. Further, SFDSA has not demonstrated that Mr. Murray had access to confidential information such that it is a basis to disqualify him as counsel in this case. SFDSA has not shown that Mr. Bosworth's representation of the Foundation is a basis to disqualify Mr. Murray in this action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MINI MINUTES:

Paul Kranz, Esq. and William Barns, Esq., of the Law Offices of Paul Kranz, Tel. 510.549.5900, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DAVID WONG, MICHAEL ZEHNER, BRIAN SAVAGE and SHEDRICK MCDANIELS'S Motion For Continued Special Motion To Strike Slapp Application For Award Of Attorney Fees Of Mulitiplier Of Two Against Johna Pecot, Thomas Arata, Tich Owyang, Stephen Tilton, Joseph Leake And Oscar Taylor is Granted in part. Attorney fees of $20,000 are awarded in favor of defendants David Wong, Michael Zehner Brian Savage, Shedrick McDaniels and the San Francisco Deputy Sheriff's Foundation and against the plaintiffs to be paid by March 30, 2012. As the prevailing party in the anti-SLAPP motion, the defendants are entitled to recover their attorney fees and costs in bringing the motion. However, the amount of the fees sought far exceeds what was reasonable to pursue the motion. Based on the court's own review of the motion, an award of $20,000 is reasonable. Mr. Murray to submit a proposed order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Defendants David Wong, Michael Zehner, Brian Savage, Shedrick Mcdaniels Motion And Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Causes Of Action In The Complaint; Request For Atty Fees And Costs Reserved For Later Motion
Off Calendar Jul-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS DAVID WONG, MICHAEL ZEHNER, BRIAN SAVAGE, and SHEDRICK MCDANIELS,and the SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S FOUNDATION'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT, REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS RESERVED FOR LATER MOTION.

APPEARANCES: Paul L. Kranz, Esq. of Law Offices of Paul L. Kranz 510/549-5900 for pltfs & opposing parties; Lawrence D. Murray, Esq. of Law Offices of Murray & Associates 673-0555 for individual defts & moving parties; Andrew J. Mailhot, Esq. of Ongard Burtt & Louderback 433-3900 for deft San Francisco Deputy Sheriff's Foundation.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission. Court ruled on defendants objections to plaintiffs evidence, sustained in part & overruled in part, order signed.
After hearing, the Court's final ruling on the motion to strike is as follows:
Defendants Special Motion to Strike the 1-5th and 7th causes of action under the anti-SLAPP statute is granted. Each of these causes of action arise from both protected petitioning/free speech activity (presenting the proposed Union dues increase for a vote of the members and inducing the Union's members to vote for the increase) and unprotected activity (misappropriation of the dues for purposes other than what was promised). Because the gravamen of these causes of action is based on nonicidental protected activity and well as unprotected activity, the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis is satisfied. Haight Ashbury Free Clinics, Inc. v. Happening House Ventures (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539.
Defendant's argument that the second prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis is met because of the preclusive effect of a dismissal of a related Federal Court lawsuit with prejudice as to these Defendants fails as to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 5th causes of action. The Court has reviewed the Federal Court action which is a derivative action asserting Federal Statutory racketeering violations based upon the same facts as asserted here. However, the 7th cause of action (Removal of Directors for Cause) is identical to one of the causes of action in the Federal Action. Because that cause of action was dismissed with prejudice as to the Defendants, it is likely plaintiffs would not prevail on this cause of action under the doctrine of Res Judicata.
Defendants further argue that Plaintiff would likely not prevail on the 1-5th causes of action as they are brought long beyond the Statute of Limitations. Plaintiffs did not address this argument in their moving papers nor at oral argument. In reviewing the complaint and the timelines provided therein, the Court agrees that these causes of action have been brought long beyond the statute of limitations. Accordingly, it is likely that the Plaintiffs will not prevail on these causes of action as well.
Mr. Murray to prepare an order reflecting the Court's decision as stated above. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
